hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 10, Q. Your opponent called your raise before the cards were dealt.
River — 8. Your opponent bet large — about the size of the pot.

wrongReasons: A 7 is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than A 7 would have.
6 8 makes a full house here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
4 3 has little reason to lead the river here — two pair can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
hand.explanation:
Answer: J J

Your opponent was drawing to a flush and called the flop to keep their options open. Once they hit, they bet the turn and river for value — growing aggression on later streets is a hallmark of a completed draw.
